title: YouTube Tightens Policies to Crack Down on Low-Quality AI Content
excerpt: Recently, YouTube officially announced a new policy to further combat AI-generated spam content on the platform. According to an update on July 16, YouTube will
content_html:
Recently, YouTube officially announced a new policy to further combat AI-generated spam content on the platform. According to an update on July 16, YouTube will classify "non-real content" into three categories and prohibit these contents from monetization. The implementation of this policy aims to maintain the platform's ecosystem and improve video quality.

The first category of content that is prohibited from monetization is generic and repetitive templated videos. These videos are usually created using AI or computer-generated images and often have no originality. If a video simply repeats existing content, it may also be affected.

The second category of content consists of videos that are offensive or distressing. These videos often intentionally create fear, pain, or emotional manipulation to gain more clicks. For example, some videos may show animals in difficult situations to attract viewers to watch subsequent rescue footage.

The third category of banned content includes videos using virtual characters to discuss sensitive topics such as health and finance. YouTube clearly states that creators should not use AI virtual personas to discuss these areas to avoid misleading viewers.

According to the new policy, if a channel publishes a large amount of the above content, it will be unable to join YouTube's Partner Program and thus lose the ability to monetize through ads. This decision reflects YouTube's emphasis on improving content quality and also responds to viewers' expectations for the quality of platform content.

With the development of AI technology, content creators face new challenges. This policy by YouTube will encourage more creators to produce high-quality, in-depth original content and reduce the spread of low-quality content.

Key Points:

- 🚫 YouTube prohibits three types of non-real content from monetization, including repetitive templates, offensive content, and videos with AI virtual characters.

- 🐾 Offensive videos intentionally create pain and fear to increase click-through rates, and the platform will strictly crack down on such content.

- 📉 Channels that post a large amount of violating content will lose the eligibility to join the Partner Program and cannot monetize through advertisements.

content_html_zh:
近日，YouTube官方宣布了一项新政策，以进一步打击平台上人工智能生成的垃圾内容。 根据7月16日的更新，YouTube将把“非真实内容”分为三类，并禁止这些内容获利。 该政策的实施旨在维护平台生态系统，提高视频质量。

第一类禁止获利的内容是通用且重复的模板视频。 这些视频通常是使用人工智能或计算机生成的图像创建的，通常没有原创性。 如果视频只是重复现有内容，也可能会受到影响。

第二类内容包括令人反感或令人痛苦的视频。 这些视频通常故意制造恐惧、痛苦或情绪操纵，以获得更多点击量。 例如，一些视频可能会展示动物处于困境的情况，以吸引观众观看后续的救援镜头。

第三类禁止内容包括使用虚拟角色讨论健康和金融等敏感话题的视频。 YouTube明确指出，创作者不应使用AI虚拟角色来讨论这些领域，以免误导观众。

根据新政策，如果频道大量发布上述内容，将无法加入YouTube的合作伙伴计划，从而失去通过广告获利的能力。 这一决定体现了YouTube对提高内容质量的重视，也回应了观众对平台内容质量的期望。

随着人工智能技术的发展，内容创作者面临新的挑战。 YouTube的这一政策将鼓励更多创作者创作高质量、有深度的原创内容，减少低质量内容的传播。

要点：

- 🚫 YouTube 禁止三类非真实内容获利，包括重复模板、攻击性内容以及带有 AI 虚拟角色的视频。

- 🐾 攻击性视频故意制造痛苦和恐惧以提高点击率，平台将严厉打击此类内容。

- 📉 大量发布违规内容的频道将失去加入合作伙伴计划的资格，并且无法通过广告获利。
